description: Frozen field-agnostic mechanism-archetype vocabulary, built blind (no benchmark access).
sources: canonical statistical mechanics (mean-field theory, critical phenomena, renormalization group)
dynamical systems and bifurcation theory (normal forms, attractors)
network science (percolation, contagion, community structure, preferential attachment)
stochastic processes (master equation, Fokker-Planck, random walks, branching processes)
nonlinear dynamics and pattern formation (reaction-diffusion, synchronization)
mathematical population dynamics and evolutionary game theory (replicator, Lotka-Volterra)
variational calculus and optimization (free energy, duality, optimal transport)
numerical and Monte-Carlo methods (MCMC, importance sampling)
linear algebra and random matrix theory (spectral decomposition, PCA)
probability limit theorems (central limit, extreme value, stable laws)
message-passing statistical inference (belief propagation, cavity method)
nLab and Wikidata structural model/theory taxonomies

archetypes.label: mean-field-order-parameter
archetypes.gloss: Each unit responds only to a population-averaged aggregate, which is in turn determined by the units' collective response; the state is found as the solution of a self-consistent fixed-point equation for that aggregate.
archetypes.provenance: canonical statistical mechanics: mean-field theory

archetypes.label: cavity-method
archetypes.gloss: A single unit's marginal behavior is computed by removing it and treating its neighbors as approximately independent, then closing the description self-consistently across all units.
archetypes.provenance: message-passing statistical inference: cavity method

archetypes.label: self-consistent-feedback-closure
archetypes.gloss: A quantity enters the equation that determines itself; the operating point is the fixed point where the imposed input equals the produced output.
archetypes.provenance: canonical statistical mechanics: self-consistent field closures

archetypes.label: annealed-vs-quenched-disorder
archetypes.gloss: Randomness is handled in two contrasting ways: fast fluctuating randomness averaged jointly with the dynamics, versus frozen randomness held fixed as a parameter and averaged only over outcomes.
archetypes.provenance: canonical statistical mechanics: disordered systems (quenched vs annealed averaging)

archetypes.label: spontaneous-symmetry-breaking
archetypes.gloss: Past a control threshold the system settles into one of several equivalent configurations, breaking a symmetry that the governing rules still respect.
archetypes.provenance: canonical statistical mechanics: symmetry breaking

archetypes.label: continuous-order-parameter-transition
archetypes.gloss: An aggregate observable rises continuously from zero as a control parameter passes a critical value, with diverging susceptibility and correlation length (second-order-like).
archetypes.provenance: canonical statistical mechanics: continuous phase transitions

archetypes.label: first-order-nucleation
archetypes.gloss: A metastable state decays through the formation of critical-sized domains that then grow; the aggregate observable jumps discontinuously and the transition shows path-dependent hysteresis.
archetypes.provenance: canonical statistical mechanics: nucleation and first-order transitions

archetypes.label: spinodal-instability
archetypes.gloss: A homogeneous state becomes linearly unstable to infinitesimal fluctuations with no barrier, decomposing spontaneously across a band of length scales.
archetypes.provenance: canonical statistical mechanics: spinodal decomposition

archetypes.label: topological-defect
archetypes.gloss: A localized singularity in an extended order field that carries a conserved integer charge and cannot be removed by any continuous, smooth deformation.
archetypes.provenance: canonical statistical mechanics / nLab: topological defects and winding number

archetypes.label: turing-instability
archetypes.gloss: A stable homogeneous state is destabilized specifically by unequal spreading rates of a short-range self-amplifying component and a long-range suppressing component, selecting a finite-wavelength stationary pattern.
archetypes.provenance: nonlinear dynamics and pattern formation: Turing/diffusion-driven instability

archetypes.label: activator-inhibitor-loop
archetypes.gloss: Short-range self-reinforcement coupled to broader-range suppression, producing spontaneous spatial or temporal structure through the competition of the two opposing ranges.
archetypes.provenance: nonlinear dynamics and pattern formation: activator-inhibitor systems

archetypes.label: traveling-wave-front
archetypes.gloss: A self-sustaining interface connecting two states propagates at a selected speed, converting one state into the other as it passes.
archetypes.provenance: nonlinear dynamics and pattern formation: reaction-diffusion fronts (Fisher-KPP)

archetypes.label: phase-separation-coarsening
archetypes.gloss: A well-mixed system spontaneously demixes into distinct domains whose characteristic size grows as a power of elapsed time (domain coarsening).
archetypes.provenance: canonical statistical mechanics: coarsening and Ostwald ripening

archetypes.label: localized-dissipative-structure
archetypes.gloss: Stable, spatially bounded structures such as isolated spots or pulses are maintained far from equilibrium by continuous throughput of energy or matter.
archetypes.provenance: nonlinear dynamics and pattern formation: dissipative solitons / localized states

archetypes.label: kinetic-interface-roughening
archetypes.gloss: A moving boundary between two regions grows rough over time, with its width and correlations obeying universal scaling exponents independent of microscopic detail.
archetypes.provenance: canonical statistical mechanics: kinetic roughening (KPZ universality)

archetypes.label: master-equation-jump-process
archetypes.gloss: Probability flows among discrete states at prescribed transition rates; each state's occupancy changes as the balance of incoming and outgoing probability fluxes.
archetypes.provenance: stochastic processes: master equation

archetypes.label: fokker-planck-drift-diffusion
archetypes.gloss: A probability density over a continuous variable evolves under a deterministic drift together with stochastic spreading, conserving total probability.
archetypes.provenance: stochastic processes: Fokker-Planck equation

archetypes.label: langevin-noise-forcing
archetypes.gloss: A deterministic equation of motion is augmented by an explicit random forcing term standing in for unresolved fast degrees of freedom.
archetypes.provenance: stochastic processes: Langevin dynamics

archetypes.label: detailed-balance-equilibrium
archetypes.gloss: A stationary state in which every microscopic transition is exactly counterbalanced by its time-reverse, so no net probability current flows in any cycle.
archetypes.provenance: canonical statistical mechanics: detailed balance / equilibrium

archetypes.label: kramers-barrier-escape
archetypes.gloss: Noise-driven crossing of a barrier separating two long-lived states occurs at an exponentially small rate set by the barrier height relative to the noise strength.
archetypes.provenance: stochastic processes: Kramers / Arrhenius activated escape

archetypes.label: first-passage-time
archetypes.gloss: Characterization of the statistics of when a stochastic process first reaches a designated boundary or target for the first time.
archetypes.provenance: stochastic processes: first-passage theory

archetypes.label: percolation-threshold
archetypes.gloss: As the density of occupied sites or links crosses a sharp critical value, a connected cluster spanning a finite fraction of the whole system appears abruptly.
archetypes.provenance: network science: percolation theory

archetypes.label: k-core-collapse
archetypes.gloss: Iterated removal of every unit with fewer than a fixed number of surviving connections triggers a discontinuous, cascading collapse of a densely interlinked subset.
archetypes.provenance: network science: k-core / bootstrap percolation

archetypes.label: bootstrap-cascade-activation
archetypes.gloss: A unit switches on once at least a threshold number of its neighbors are on; a small initial seed can therefore trigger an all-or-nothing global activation.
archetypes.provenance: network science: bootstrap percolation / threshold activation

archetypes.label: giant-connected-component
archetypes.gloss: Emergence of a single cluster that occupies an extensive (system-spanning) fraction of all units once connectivity exceeds a critical mean degree.
archetypes.provenance: network science: random-graph giant component

archetypes.label: belief-propagation
archetypes.gloss: Local estimates are passed iteratively along the edges of a graph and combined at each node to approximate global marginals; exact when the graph is a tree.
archetypes.provenance: message-passing statistical inference: belief propagation / sum-product

archetypes.label: unbiased-random-walk
archetypes.gloss: A path built from increments with zero mean, so typical displacement grows only as the square root of the number of steps (diffusive spreading).
archetypes.provenance: stochastic processes: random walk / diffusion

archetypes.label: martingale-fair-process
archetypes.gloss: A stochastic process whose expected future value, conditioned on all information so far, equals its present value (no predictable drift).
archetypes.provenance: stochastic processes: martingale theory

archetypes.label: diffusion-limited-aggregation
archetypes.gloss: Randomly wandering units stick irreversibly on contact with a growing cluster, building branched, self-similar fractal structures.
archetypes.provenance: stochastic processes: diffusion-limited aggregation

archetypes.label: levy-flight-superdiffusion
archetypes.gloss: A walk whose step lengths are drawn from a heavy-tailed distribution, producing scale-free excursions and spreading faster than ordinary diffusion.
archetypes.provenance: stochastic processes: Levy flights / anomalous diffusion

archetypes.label: extreme-value-statistics
archetypes.gloss: The statistics of the largest or smallest values, and of successive record-breaking events, drawn from many samples, governed by universal limiting laws.
archetypes.provenance: probability limit theorems: extreme value theory

archetypes.label: replicator-selection
archetypes.gloss: The growth rate of each type is proportional to how far its performance exceeds the population average, so above-average types expand and below-average ones shrink.
archetypes.provenance: evolutionary game theory: replicator dynamics

archetypes.label: logistic-saturation
archetypes.gloss: Self-reinforcing growth that decelerates and levels off as a finite carrying capacity is approached, giving an S-shaped trajectory.
archetypes.provenance: mathematical population dynamics: logistic growth

archetypes.label: consumer-resource-cycle
archetypes.gloss: Two coupled quantities, one feeding on the other, generate sustained out-of-phase oscillations in which each rises and falls after the other.
archetypes.provenance: mathematical population dynamics: Lotka-Volterra consumer-resource oscillation

archetypes.label: competitive-exclusion
archetypes.gloss: When several types draw on a single limiting shared quantity, all but the most efficient are eventually driven out.
archetypes.provenance: mathematical population dynamics: competitive exclusion principle

archetypes.label: mutualistic-facilitation
archetypes.gloss: Reciprocal positive coupling in which each interacting type raises the abundance or performance of the other.
archetypes.provenance: mathematical population dynamics: mutualism / facilitation

archetypes.label: frequency-dependent-selection
archetypes.gloss: A type's success depends on how common it already is, which can either stabilize coexistence or drive cyclic turnover.
archetypes.provenance: evolutionary game theory: frequency-dependent selection

archetypes.label: evolutionary-stable-strategy
archetypes.gloss: A prevailing configuration that, once established, cannot be displaced by any rare invading alternative.
archetypes.provenance: evolutionary game theory: evolutionarily stable strategy

archetypes.label: autocatalytic-self-amplification
archetypes.gloss: A component promotes its own production, so its abundance grows in proportion to itself, giving self-reinforcing exponential amplification.
archetypes.provenance: canonical reaction kinetics / TRIZ principle 25 (self-service): autocatalysis

archetypes.label: mass-action-bilinear-coupling
archetypes.gloss: The rate of an interaction is proportional to the product of the densities of the two interacting populations, giving a bilinear encounter term.
archetypes.provenance: canonical reaction kinetics: law of mass action

archetypes.label: renormalization-group-flow
archetypes.gloss: Repeated coarse-graining generates a flow through parameter space; its fixed points and their stability dictate the large-scale behavior of every member of a family.
archetypes.provenance: canonical statistical mechanics: renormalization group

archetypes.label: scale-invariance-power-law
archetypes.gloss: The absence of any characteristic scale, so that observables reproduce themselves under rescaling and follow power-law distributions.
archetypes.provenance: canonical statistical mechanics: scale invariance and power laws

archetypes.label: data-collapse-scaling-function
archetypes.gloss: Rescaling the axes by appropriate powers of the distance to a critical point collapses many distinct curves onto a single universal master function.
archetypes.provenance: canonical statistical mechanics: finite-size scaling / data collapse

archetypes.label: universality-class
archetypes.gloss: Systems with entirely different microscopics share identical large-scale exponents, determined only by symmetry, conservation laws, and dimensionality.
archetypes.provenance: canonical statistical mechanics: universality

archetypes.label: coarse-graining-effective-theory
archetypes.gloss: Integrating out fast or short-scale degrees of freedom yields reduced equations that describe only the slow, large-scale variables.
archetypes.provenance: canonical statistical mechanics: effective theory / coarse-graining

archetypes.label: compartmental-flow-model
archetypes.gloss: A conserved total is partitioned into discrete compartments, and units transfer between compartments at prescribed rates while the sum stays fixed.
archetypes.provenance: mathematical epidemiology / systems dynamics: compartmental models

archetypes.label: threshold-reproduction-number
archetypes.gloss: A spreading process grows or dies out depending on whether the average number of secondary units generated per unit exceeds one.
archetypes.provenance: mathematical epidemiology: basic reproduction number threshold

archetypes.label: absorbing-state-transition
archetypes.gloss: A dynamics containing a trap it can never leave; an active, fluctuating phase survives indefinitely only when a control parameter exceeds a critical value.
archetypes.provenance: canonical statistical mechanics: absorbing-state phase transitions (directed percolation)

archetypes.label: birth-death-branching
archetypes.gloss: Units independently generate copies and are removed at fixed per-unit rates; ultimate extinction versus unbounded growth is set by the mean offspring number.
archetypes.provenance: stochastic processes: branching / birth-death processes

archetypes.label: saddle-node-bifurcation
archetypes.gloss: As a parameter crosses a threshold, two fixed points (one stable, one unstable) collide and annihilate, so a steady state abruptly appears or vanishes.
archetypes.provenance: dynamical systems: saddle-node (fold) bifurcation

archetypes.label: transcritical-exchange-of-stability
archetypes.gloss: Two fixed points pass through each other at a parameter value and swap their stability without disappearing.
archetypes.provenance: dynamical systems: transcritical bifurcation

archetypes.label: pitchfork-symmetric-branching
archetypes.gloss: A symmetric fixed point loses stability and gives birth to a symmetric pair of new stable states on either side.
archetypes.provenance: dynamical systems: pitchfork bifurcation

archetypes.label: hopf-bifurcation
archetypes.gloss: A steady state loses stability to a growing oscillation, giving birth to a self-sustained limit cycle of finite amplitude.
archetypes.provenance: dynamical systems: Hopf bifurcation

archetypes.label: period-doubling-cascade
archetypes.gloss: Successive doublings of an oscillation's period accumulate at a finite parameter value, opening a route into aperiodic, chaotic motion.
archetypes.provenance: dynamical systems: period-doubling route to chaos

archetypes.label: excitable-threshold-response
archetypes.gloss: Small perturbations decay quietly, but any perturbation above a threshold triggers a large stereotyped excursion followed by a refractory return to rest.
archetypes.provenance: dynamical systems: excitable media (FitzHugh-Nagumo type)

archetypes.label: relaxation-oscillation
archetypes.gloss: A cycle of slow buildup punctuated by fast discharge, arising when the dynamics has two widely separated timescales.
archetypes.provenance: dynamical systems: relaxation oscillations (van der Pol type)

archetypes.label: bistability-hysteresis
archetypes.gloss: Two stable states coexist over a range of a control parameter, and which one is realized depends on history, giving a hysteresis loop under slow cycling.
archetypes.provenance: dynamical systems: bistability and hysteresis

archetypes.label: critical-slowing-down
archetypes.gloss: The time to recover from a perturbation lengthens without bound as a threshold is approached, serving as an early-warning signature of an imminent transition.
archetypes.provenance: dynamical systems / statistical mechanics: critical slowing down

archetypes.label: tipping-point-catastrophe
archetypes.gloss: A gradual change in a control parameter triggers a sudden, often irreversible jump to a far-away alternative state as a fold is crossed.
archetypes.provenance: dynamical systems: catastrophe / fold tipping points

archetypes.label: free-energy-minimization
archetypes.gloss: The selected configuration minimizes a functional that trades an energy-like cost term against an entropy-like spread term.
archetypes.provenance: canonical statistical mechanics / variational calculus: free-energy minimization

archetypes.label: variational-stationary-action
archetypes.gloss: The realized trajectory or configuration is the one that makes a scalar functional stationary (extremal) against all nearby alternatives.
archetypes.provenance: variational calculus: principle of stationary action

archetypes.label: maximum-entropy-inference
archetypes.gloss: Among all distributions consistent with the known constraints, the least-biased one maximizes an entropy functional.
archetypes.provenance: information theory: maximum-entropy principle

archetypes.label: constrained-lagrange-multiplier
archetypes.gloss: An objective is extremized subject to constraints by introducing dual multiplier variables that price each constraint at its shadow value.
archetypes.provenance: variational calculus / optimization: Lagrange multipliers

archetypes.label: primal-dual-duality
archetypes.gloss: A hard optimization is recast as an equivalent complementary problem whose solution bounds and mirrors the original.
archetypes.provenance: optimization: primal-dual / Legendre duality

archetypes.label: simple-contagion-spread
archetypes.gloss: A state transmits independently across each active contact, so a single exposure can suffice to convert a neighbor.
archetypes.provenance: network science: simple contagion

archetypes.label: complex-contagion-reinforcement
archetypes.gloss: Adoption requires multiple reinforcing exposures from several distinct neighbors rather than a single contact.
archetypes.provenance: network science: complex contagion

archetypes.label: threshold-cascade-network
archetypes.gloss: Each node switches once the fraction of its already-switched neighbors exceeds its own threshold, allowing a small seed to trigger a global cascade.
archetypes.provenance: network science: linear threshold / Watts cascade model

archetypes.label: hub-dominated-spreading
archetypes.gloss: Broad heterogeneity in connectivity makes spreading dominated by a few very highly connected units, lowering or erasing the effective threshold.
archetypes.provenance: network science: spreading on heterogeneous (scale-free) networks

archetypes.label: imitation-consensus-dynamics
archetypes.gloss: Each unit repeatedly copies the state of a randomly chosen neighbor, driving the population toward uniform consensus or coarsening domains.
archetypes.provenance: network science / statistical mechanics: voter model

archetypes.label: self-organized-criticality
archetypes.gloss: Slow driving combined with threshold-triggered relaxation pulls a system, without external tuning, to a critical state emitting scale-free bursts of all sizes.
archetypes.provenance: self-organized criticality: Bak-Tang-Wiesenfeld paradigm

archetypes.label: load-redistribution-avalanche
archetypes.gloss: When a locally accumulated quantity exceeds a threshold it discharges onto neighbors, which may in turn exceed threshold, yielding events with a power-law size distribution.
archetypes.provenance: self-organized criticality: sandpile / avalanche dynamics

archetypes.label: punctuated-equilibrium
archetypes.gloss: Long stretches of near-stasis are interrupted by rare, sudden bursts of rapid reorganization.
archetypes.provenance: self-organized criticality / evolutionary dynamics: punctuated equilibrium

archetypes.label: gradient-descent-flow
archetypes.gloss: The state moves continuously in the direction of steepest decrease of a scalar potential, coming to rest at local minima.
archetypes.provenance: optimization / dynamical systems: gradient flow

archetypes.label: rugged-landscape-metastability
archetypes.gloss: A high-dimensional cost surface riddled with many local minima separated by barriers traps the dynamics in long-lived metastable configurations.
archetypes.provenance: canonical statistical mechanics: rugged energy landscapes

archetypes.label: annealing-noise-schedule
archetypes.gloss: An effective noise level is lowered gradually so the system escapes shallow traps early and settles into deep low-cost states as noise fades.
archetypes.provenance: optimization: simulated annealing

archetypes.label: basin-of-attraction
archetypes.gloss: The set of starting states that all flow to a given attractor; basin boundaries partition state space into competing outcomes.
archetypes.provenance: dynamical systems: basins of attraction

archetypes.label: minimum-energy-path-saddle
archetypes.gloss: Transitions between two minima proceed through the lowest connecting saddle, and that saddle's height controls the transition rate.
archetypes.provenance: dynamical systems / chemical kinetics: minimum-energy path and transition state

archetypes.label: conservation-continuity-equation
archetypes.gloss: A locally conserved quantity changes only by flux across boundaries, so its local accumulation equals the net inflow.
archetypes.provenance: canonical field theory: continuity / conservation law

archetypes.label: flux-balance-steady-state
archetypes.gloss: At steady state the inflow into every node exactly equals its outflow, so all stocks remain constant while flows persist.
archetypes.provenance: systems dynamics: flux balance / stationary flow

archetypes.label: gradient-driven-transport
archetypes.gloss: A flow is driven in proportion to the gradient of a potential, moving the conserved quantity from high toward low (linear-response transport).
archetypes.provenance: canonical transport theory: Fick/Fourier/Ohm linear-response laws

archetypes.label: noether-symmetry-conservation
archetypes.gloss: Every continuous symmetry of the governing functional implies a corresponding conserved quantity.
archetypes.provenance: canonical field theory: Noether's theorem

archetypes.label: phase-oscillator-synchronization
archetypes.gloss: A population of coupled cyclic units adjusts their rhythms until, above a critical coupling, a macroscopic fraction locks onto a common phase.
archetypes.provenance: nonlinear dynamics: Kuramoto synchronization transition

archetypes.label: entrainment-to-external-drive
archetypes.gloss: An autonomous oscillator locks its frequency to a periodic external forcing whenever the forcing lies within a finite locking band.
archetypes.provenance: nonlinear dynamics: entrainment / phase locking (Arnold tongues)

archetypes.label: chimera-partial-synchrony
archetypes.gloss: In an otherwise symmetric population, a synchronized subgroup coexists persistently with a desynchronized subgroup.
archetypes.provenance: nonlinear dynamics: chimera states

archetypes.label: negative-feedback-regulation
archetypes.gloss: The output is fed back to oppose deviations, holding the system near a set point and rejecting disturbances.
archetypes.provenance: control theory: negative feedback regulation

archetypes.label: positive-feedback-amplification
archetypes.gloss: The output reinforces its own cause, producing runaway growth or sharp switch-like transitions between states.
archetypes.provenance: control theory: positive feedback

archetypes.label: integral-feedback-adaptation
archetypes.gloss: Accumulating the running error over time and feeding it back drives the steady-state deviation exactly to zero, giving perfect adaptation.
archetypes.provenance: control theory: integral feedback / perfect adaptation

archetypes.label: delayed-feedback-oscillation
archetypes.gloss: Feedback applied with a time lag can destabilize an otherwise stable steady state and convert it into sustained oscillation.
archetypes.provenance: control theory / dynamical systems: delayed feedback instability

archetypes.label: proportional-restoring-force
archetypes.gloss: A restoring response proportional to displacement from equilibrium produces oscillation about that equilibrium at a natural frequency.
archetypes.provenance: canonical mechanics: linear restoring force / harmonic oscillator

archetypes.label: channel-capacity-bound
archetypes.gloss: A hard upper limit on the rate of reliable transmission through a noisy medium, set by the ratio of signal to noise.
archetypes.provenance: information theory: Shannon channel capacity

archetypes.label: rate-distortion-tradeoff
archetypes.gloss: The minimum amount of information needed to represent a source grows as the tolerated reconstruction error shrinks, tracing a fundamental tradeoff curve.
archetypes.provenance: information theory: rate-distortion theory

archetypes.label: information-bottleneck
archetypes.gloss: A variable is compressed as much as possible while retaining only the part of it that is predictive of a designated target.
archetypes.provenance: information theory: information bottleneck

archetypes.label: fluctuation-dissipation-link
archetypes.gloss: The average response to a small perturbation is tied quantitatively to the spontaneous fluctuations the system exhibits at rest.
archetypes.provenance: canonical statistical mechanics: fluctuation-dissipation theorem

archetypes.label: mutual-information-coupling
archetypes.gloss: A symmetric measure of statistical dependence quantifying how much knowing one subsystem reduces uncertainty about another.
archetypes.provenance: information theory: mutual information

archetypes.label: importance-sampling-reweighting
archetypes.gloss: Averages under a hard-to-sample distribution are estimated by drawing from an easier one and correcting each sample by a likelihood-ratio weight.
archetypes.provenance: numerical and Monte-Carlo methods: importance sampling

archetypes.label: markov-chain-monte-carlo
archetypes.gloss: A stochastic walk is engineered so that its long-run stationary distribution is the desired target, and averages are taken along the trajectory.
archetypes.provenance: numerical and Monte-Carlo methods: Markov chain Monte Carlo

archetypes.label: metropolis-acceptance-rule
archetypes.gloss: Proposed moves are accepted or rejected with a probability crafted so that the target distribution is left invariant.
archetypes.provenance: numerical and Monte-Carlo methods: Metropolis-Hastings acceptance

archetypes.label: rejection-acceptance-sampling
archetypes.gloss: Candidates are drawn from a simple envelope distribution and a fraction accepted so that survivors follow the target density.
archetypes.provenance: numerical and Monte-Carlo methods: rejection sampling

archetypes.label: spectral-eigenmode-decomposition
archetypes.gloss: Structure or dynamics is re-expressed in the basis of eigenvectors of an operator, with behavior dominated by the largest eigenvalues.
archetypes.provenance: linear algebra: eigen-decomposition / normal modes

archetypes.label: dominant-eigenvector-centrality
archetypes.gloss: The stationary importance of each unit is given by the leading eigenvector of the interaction matrix, so importance flows recursively from important neighbors.
archetypes.provenance: linear algebra / network science: eigenvector centrality (Perron-Frobenius)

archetypes.label: spectral-gap-relaxation
archetypes.gloss: The separation between the top two eigenvalues of the evolution operator sets the slowest relaxation or mixing timescale.
archetypes.provenance: linear algebra / stochastic processes: spectral gap and mixing time

archetypes.label: low-rank-factorization
archetypes.gloss: A large interaction array is approximated as the product of a few latent factors, revealing that its effective dimensionality is small.
archetypes.provenance: linear algebra: low-rank / latent-factor factorization

archetypes.label: principal-component-projection
archetypes.gloss: High-dimensional variation is projected onto the few orthogonal directions that capture the greatest variance.
archetypes.provenance: linear algebra: principal component analysis

archetypes.label: random-matrix-spectral-statistics
archetypes.gloss: The eigenvalues of large disordered interaction arrays follow universal distributions with characteristic bulk laws, edge behavior, and level repulsion.
archetypes.provenance: random matrix theory: universal spectral statistics

archetypes.label: graph-laplacian-diffusion
archetypes.gloss: Spreading and smoothing over a network are governed by the graph Laplacian, driving connected units toward local agreement (heat flow / consensus).
archetypes.provenance: network science / spectral graph theory: Laplacian diffusion

archetypes.label: central-limit-gaussian-aggregation
archetypes.gloss: A sum of many independent, comparably sized contributions converges to a bell-shaped Gaussian regardless of the individual details.
archetypes.provenance: probability limit theorems: central limit theorem

archetypes.label: stable-law-heavy-tail-aggregation
archetypes.gloss: When contributions have heavy tails with no finite variance, their sums converge instead to heavy-tailed stable distributions (generalized limit law).
archetypes.provenance: probability limit theorems: stable laws / generalized central limit theorem

archetypes.label: bayesian-belief-updating
archetypes.gloss: A prior distribution is multiplied by the likelihood of new evidence and renormalized to a posterior, integrating information sequentially.
archetypes.provenance: probability / statistical inference: Bayesian updating

archetypes.label: small-world-shortcuts
archetypes.gloss: A few long-range links added to a locally clustered structure collapse typical path lengths while preserving high local clustering.
archetypes.provenance: network science: small-world networks

archetypes.label: preferential-attachment-growth
archetypes.gloss: Newly added units connect preferentially to already well-connected ones, so connectivity begets connectivity and heavy-tailed degrees emerge.
archetypes.provenance: network science: preferential attachment / cumulative advantage

archetypes.label: scale-free-degree-distribution
archetypes.gloss: The distribution of connectivity across units is heavy-tailed with no typical scale, so a few units have enormously many links.
archetypes.provenance: network science: scale-free degree distributions

archetypes.label: modular-community-structure
archetypes.gloss: Units partition into densely interconnected groups that are only sparsely coupled to one another.
archetypes.provenance: network science: community / modular structure

archetypes.label: separation-of-timescales-averaging
archetypes.gloss: When some variables move much faster than others, one averages over the fast motion to obtain closed effective equations for the slow variables.
archetypes.provenance: dynamical systems: multiple-timescale averaging / adiabatic elimination

archetypes.label: slow-manifold-reduction
archetypes.gloss: Fast degrees of freedom rapidly collapse onto a low-dimensional invariant surface on which the remaining slow dynamics unfolds.
archetypes.provenance: dynamical systems: slow manifold / center manifold reduction

archetypes.label: mode-coupling-cascade
archetypes.gloss: Nonlinear interactions transfer excitation systematically from one scale to another, redistributing a conserved input across a range of scales (energy cascade).
archetypes.provenance: nonlinear dynamics: turbulent cascades / mode coupling

archetypes.label: resonance-amplification
archetypes.gloss: A driven system responds with disproportionately large amplitude when the driving frequency matches one of its internal natural frequencies.
archetypes.provenance: canonical mechanics: resonance

archetypes.label: stochastic-resonance
archetypes.gloss: An intermediate, nonzero noise level maximizes a system's response to a weak periodic signal, so some randomness helps rather than hurts detection.
archetypes.provenance: nonlinear dynamics / statistical physics: stochastic resonance

archetypes.label: bottleneck-rate-limiting-step
archetypes.gloss: The overall throughput of a sequential multi-stage process is dictated almost entirely by its single slowest stage.
archetypes.provenance: chemical kinetics / systems dynamics: rate-limiting step

archetypes.label: optimal-transport-matching
archetypes.gloss: One distribution is reshaped into another by moving mass along the assignment that minimizes total transport cost.
archetypes.provenance: optimization: optimal transport / Monge-Kantorovich

archetypes.label: game-theoretic-equilibrium
archetypes.gloss: A configuration of mutually best responses from which no self-interested unit can gain by unilaterally changing its choice.
archetypes.provenance: game theory: Nash equilibrium

archetypes.label: tragedy-of-shared-resource
archetypes.gloss: Individually rational exploitation of a common shared pool leads collectively to its depletion and a worse outcome for all.
archetypes.provenance: game theory: commons dilemma / social trap

archetypes.label: frustration-competing-constraints
archetypes.gloss: Constraints that cannot all be satisfied at once force the system into many nearly degenerate compromise configurations.
archetypes.provenance: canonical statistical mechanics: geometric frustration

archetypes.label: glassy-slow-relaxation
archetypes.gloss: Dynamics slow down dramatically and keep aging without ever reaching equilibrium, trapped by a rugged landscape of nearly equivalent traps.
archetypes.provenance: canonical statistical mechanics: glassy dynamics and aging

archetypes.label: cellular-automaton-local-update
archetypes.gloss: Discrete units on a lattice update in parallel according to a fixed local rule depending only on their neighbors, generating global structure from local interaction.
archetypes.provenance: dynamical systems / complex systems: cellular automata

archetypes.label: intermediary-mediator
archetypes.gloss: An interaction between two parts is carried by an inserted intermediate agent or carrier that couples them without being permanently consumed.
archetypes.provenance: TRIZ principle 24 (intermediary) / canonical catalysis: mediator coupling
